commit.message:
Only show idle tip when microphone permission isn't already granted (#2)

The "Press Start to begin" tip below the meter card was shown to all
idle users, including returning users who've already granted mic access
and don't need the prompt.

## Changes

- **New state** (`micPermissionGranted`): queries
`navigator.permissions` for `'microphone'` on mount; defaults to `false`
so the tip remains visible when the API is unsupported
- **Live sync**: attaches a `change` listener on the `PermissionStatus`
object so the tip disappears immediately if permission is granted
mid-session
- **Safe cleanup**: uses a `mounted` flag to guard against state updates
and dangling listeners when the component unmounts before the async
query resolves
- **Updated render condition**: tip now requires `!micPermissionGranted`
in addition to the existing `phase === 'idle' && !permissionDenied &&
!sessionEnded` guards

```tsx
// Before
{phase === 'idle' && !permissionDenied && !sessionEnded && (

// After
{phase === 'idle' && !permissionDenied && !sessionEnded && !micPermissionGranted && (
```

commit.message:
Resolve build errors, enhance document handling, and update UI states (#235)

* fix(id-verification, notifications): resolve build error and SSE timeout

- Remove duplicate writeTerminalVerificationStatus import from app/actions/verify-id;
  use only the lib/services/identity-verification-terminal version (no "use server")
- Remove duplicate onFailure logic with undeclared variables; delegate entirely
  to handleAutoVerifyIdentityRetryExhausted which already handles terminal write,
  logging, and notification with proper .catch() guards
- Fix step.run outcome handling: replace undeclared hasError/isNonRetriableError
  with explicit outcome.kind checks matching IdentityStepOutcome discriminated union
- Remove unused isNonRetriableIdentityError import
- Add 280s server-side max-age timeout to /api/notifications/stream so Vercel's
  300s function limit is never hit; browser EventSource reconnects automatically

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove .vscode/settings.json file

- Deleted the Visual Studio Code settings file to streamline project configuration and avoid unnecessary clutter in the repository.

* chore: update dependencies and configuration

- Updated various package versions in package.json and bun.lock for improved stability and features, including @google/genai, @mendable/firecrawl-js, and @clerk/testing.
- Modified database migration commands to use bunx for consistency.
- Removed outdated markdown files related to PostHog setup and human-agent interaction design.
- Adjusted test data in form content for better accuracy.
- Enhanced error handling and logging in control tower functions to improve reliability.
- Introduced new SQL migration file for index management.

* refactor: update risk review report and document upload handling

- Removed unused imports and optimized database queries in the risk review report page.
- Enhanced the document upload API to include workflow ID resolution and improved error handling for unsupported file types.
- Introduced a mapping function for document categories to streamline uploads.
- Updated the FICA and ITC sections to display bank statement analysis status and warnings.
- Added live data fetching for risk review details to improve user experience and data accuracy.

* refactor: transition from documents to documentUploads in identity verification and related APIs

- Replaced references to the `documents` table with `documentUploads` across various files to streamline document handling.
- Updated the identity verification process to fetch documents exclusively from the `documentUploads` table, enhancing data integrity and consistency.
- Modified API endpoints to support the new document structure, ensuring compatibility with existing workflows.
- Removed unused imports and optimized database queries for improved performance.
- Introduced new SQL migration to reflect the schema changes and ensure proper database structure.

* feat: implement comprehensive migration to document_uploads and enhance UI states

- Introduced a detailed migration plan to transition from the legacy documents table to document_uploads, ensuring a single source of truth for document handling.
- Enhanced the backend to support deterministic date validation, eliminating hallucinated temporal judgments by implementing Gemini Date Grounding.
- Updated the UI to provide explicit states for bank-statement analysis, ensuring users always see actionable statuses.
- Added a supplemental FICA enrichment UI to surface full validation payloads in risk-review details, improving visibility and auditability.
- Implemented real-time updates and improved error handling across various components to enhance user experience and data accuracy.
- Conducted thorough verification steps to ensure all changes meet success criteria and maintain system integrity.

* refactor: replace button with Button component in notifications panel

- Updated the NotificationsPanel component to use a custom Button component instead of a standard button element for improved styling and consistency.
- Adjusted class names for better alignment with design specifications.
- Ensured accessibility features remain intact with appropriate screen reader text.

* fix: correct typo in db:studio command in package.json

- Updated the command from "bunx rizzle-kit studio" to "bunx drizzle-kit studio" to ensure proper functionality and consistency in database studio operations.

* fix: clarify amount representation in quote service documentation

- Updated the documentation in quote.service.ts to specify that the amount is represented in cents (ZAR) instead of just integer (ZAR), enhancing clarity for users regarding the expected format.

* generatedBy: "Google Gemini"

* Update app/(authenticated)/dashboard/risk-review/reports/[id]/page.tsx

commit.message:
fix(ci): feature-gate PyO3 so server binary links on macOS (#44)

* fix(ci): feature-gate PyO3 so server binary links on macOS

On macOS the linker is strict about undefined symbols. PyO3's
extension-module feature emits -undefined dynamic_lookup only for
cdylib targets, not for bin targets, so the server binary link fails
with unresolved Python C-API symbols.

Fix: make pyo3 and numpy optional behind a default-enabled 'python'
Cargo feature. server/Cargo.toml now uses default-features = false,
so the server never pulls in PyO3 and links cleanly on all platforms.

Also:
- Remove unused pyo3-build-config build-dependency (no build.rs)
- Add CI check for server build (cargo check server/Cargo.toml)
- Update release.yml macOS comments to reflect the real fix

Optimize video playback latency: fire-and-forget engine stop in previewVideo

The await enginePlaybackStop() in previewVideo serialized a full CoreAudio
device close (~200-500ms) before enginePlaybackStart, which internally calls
stopOutputLocked() anyway — doubling the device-close cost.

With the C++ generation counter (loadGen/consumedGen) now protecting
sessionPath from stale playback_stop clears, this explicit stop no longer
needs to be awaited.  Making it fire-and-forget saves ~200-500ms of video
playback startup latency.

commit.message:
fix: Azure tool serialization crash causing 100% CLI fallback

tool.parameters is a voluptuous Schema, not a dict. It was being passed
directly to the JSON serializer, causing 'Object of type Schema is not
JSON serializable' on every Azure-routed request. All Azure calls failed
at ~3ms and fell back to CLI.

Fix: use voluptuous_openapi.convert() with the LLM API's custom_serializer
to properly convert tool schemas to JSON-compatible dicts, matching how
HA's built-in conversation integrations (OpenRouter, Google AI, etc.)
handle this.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(db): use SECURITY DEFINER helper for Storage upload policy

The anon role cannot query devices table (RLS blocks it), so the
EXISTS subquery always returns false. Using a SECURITY DEFINER
function bypasses RLS, matching the pattern of all other device RPCs.
